headline: US Keeps Hormuz Open as Israel Strikes Hezbollah, Ukraine Faces New Bomb Wave
subheadline: U.S. forces secure passage in the Strait of Hormuz while Israeli jets kill Hezbollah operatives in Lebanon; Russia launches guided bombs across Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia.
lede: U.S. Central Command confirmed the Strait of Hormuz remains open for commercial traffic, with forces positioned to deter Iranian aggression. Israel reported multiple strikes in southern Lebanon, killing several Hezbollah operatives and targeting a tunnel near Beaufort Castle. Meanwhile, Russian tactical aviation deployed guided bombs across Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, while UAVs surged over multiple Ukrainian oblasts.

sections.title: Russian Air Strikes Heighten Pressure on Ukrainian Front
sections.body: Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and eastern Kharkiv regions. UAVs were observed moving from Sumy toward Nizhyn, from the north toward Poltava, and over Pavlohrad heading north. Additional UAV incursions entered Dnipropetrovsk, Donetsk, Odesa and Synelnykivka districts with varied headings. Alerts warned of possible air strikes on southeastern, eastern and southern front‑line areas.

sections.title: Israel Expands Operations Amid Hormuz Tensions
sections.body: An explosion was reported near Sirik in Iran's Hormozgan Province, confirmed by state media. U.S. forces maintain open transit in the Strait of Hormuz, positioning assets to deter Iranian aggression. Israeli air force struck and killed several Hezbollah operatives fleeing a tunnel near Beaufort Castle and bombed an area between Arnoun and Kfar Tebnit. A drone also targeted Hezbollah operatives in Deir al-Balah, Gaza.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: The data shows a simultaneous escalation: U.S. naval presence secures a critical maritime chokepoint while Israel intensifies strikes against Hezbollah, indicating coordinated pressure on Iranian-aligned forces. Russia's widespread guided‑bomb and UAV activity across multiple Ukrainian fronts suggests preparation for broader coordinated air operations.
